message:
feat(daemon): operational improvements for production stability and scan efficiency

Addresses several issues observed on production machines (trj) including
unbounded walker memory growth (14GB+ RSS), excessive circuit breaker
tripping from FUSE mount errors, and scan timeout re-walking of barren
directory subtrees.

Config tuning:
- Increase pressure poll interval from 1s to 5s (reduces CPU overhead)
- Lower min_file_age from 10min to 5min (faster cleanup under pressure)
- Extend scan_time_budget from 300s to 600s (fewer timeouts on large trees)
- Raise circuit_breaker_threshold from 3 to 5 (tolerate transient errors)

Monitoring daemon (loop_main.rs):
- Add 5-minute periodic summary reports with scan/deletion/freed metrics
  and process RSS tracking via /proc/self/status
- Implement ScanCursor for incremental scanning: after a timed-out scan,
  barren directories (zero candidates) are cached and excluded from the
  next pass, allowing the walker to explore new territory instead of
  re-walking known-empty subtrees (30-min TTL, 50K entry cap)
- Fast-track all classified build artifacts under Orange+ pressure
  (RustTarget, BuildOutput, CacheDir, AgentWorkspace, TempDir) since
  the open-file check in the executor is the real safety net
- Skip scan enqueue under Green/Yellow when channel is already full
  to eliminate "scan channel saturated" log noise
- Bound walker result channel from unbounded to 10,000 entries to
  prevent runaway memory growth on large directory trees
- Track visited_dirs and dirs_with_candidates for cursor updates

Executor improvements:
- Exponential backoff for circuit breaker cooldown (double on each trip,
  cap at 5 minutes, reset to base on successful deletion)
- Rate-limit circuit breaker cooldown log messages to once per 60s
- Reset consecutive failure counter on skip (a skipped candidate is not
  a failure; prevents FUSE mount errors from tripping the breaker for
  unrelated /tmp deletions)

Policy engine:
- Auto-recalibrate after 100 consecutive breach windows (previously
  accumulated unboundedly, seen reaching 1296+ on production machines);
  resets counter and logs recalibration notice so the guard re-learns
  from fresh observations

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
